comment_text.sn19.13:1.4: Contact, or “touch”, is like a flayed cow (SN 12.63:4.2), so the touches she formerly experienced as pleasant are now excruciating.

root_text.sn19.13:1.1: “Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasaṁ nicchaviṁ itthiṁ vehāsaṁ gacchantiṁ.
root_text.sn19.13:1.2: Tamenaṁ gijjhāpi kākāpi kulalāpi anupatitvā anupatitvā vitacchenti virājenti.
root_text.sn19.13:1.4: esā, bhikkhave, itthī imasmiṁyeva rājagahe aticārinī ahosi …pe….

translation_text.sn19.13:1.1: “Just now, reverend, as I was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain I saw a flayed woman flying through the air.
translation_text.sn19.13:1.2: Vultures, crows, and hawks kept chasing her, pecking and clawing
translation_text.sn19.13:1.4: “That woman used to be an adulteress right here in Rājagaha. …”
